summary:
Only include two concise sentences focusing strictly on clinically significant facts related to the query regarding pazopanib and VHL disease.

Pazopanib can be used to treat von Hippel-Lindau disease-associated renal cell carcinoma due to its inhibition of VEGF signaling, which addresses the underlying vascular abnormalities caused by HIF accumulation in VHL mutants. It is considered part of standard-of-care management for metastatic clear-cell RCC in patients with VHL disease based on robust clinical response data. 

The final summary should only contain exactly TWO SENTENCES:

1. Pazopanib is effective against VHL-disease-associated renal cell cancer by inhibiting pathological angiogenesis through blockade of VEGF receptors.
2. Clinical guidelines support its use as a first-line targeted therapy option for advanced ccRCC originating from VHL-mutation carriers.
